title: deschampsia media subsp. hispanica
intro: Deschampsia media subsp. hispanica, also known as Spanish Hair Grass, is a perennial grass species native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant is rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have potential health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, and may help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. Traditionally, Deschampsia media subsp. hispanica has been prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve, and has been used in herbal medicine to treat a range of conditions, including colds, fever, and skin irritations.
